name: Chicken with Ginger, Broccoli, and Grapefruit
recipeIngredient: pared and cut into match-stick strips
skinned and cut crosswise into 1/2-inch strips
recipeInstructions:
1. In a 10-inch skillet, heat oil over medium heat.
2. Cook ginger, stirring occasionally, until golden brown, about 2-3 minutes.
3. Remove ginger to plate; set aside.
4. Increase heat to medium-high. Add chicken and cook, stirring constantly, until no longer pink and cooked through, about 3 minutes. Remove chicken to plate; set aside.
5. Add broccoli and stir to coat with cooking liquids.
6. Add 1/4 cup water to the skillet; cover and cook until crisp-tender, 3 to 4 minutes.
7. Return chicken to skillet. Add broth, grapefruit juice, and soy sauce.
8. In a small bowl, combine cornstarch with 1 tablespoon cold water until cornstarch is dissolved.
9. Add cornstarch mixture to the skillet; increase heat to high.
10. Cook, stirring frequently, until mixture boils and thickens slightly.
11. Add grapefruit sections to the skillet; heat through.
12. Spoon chicken and broccoli mixture onto a serving platter.
13. Garnish with reserved ginger.
Each serving provides: 1 fat, 3 protein, 1 vegetable, 1/2 fruit, 10 carbs. Per serving: 221 calories. Source: Weight Watchers December 1993 Magazine. Shared by Judi M. Phelps.
